customManagers.description: Track build-system and dependency pins inside solx-dev harness configs. The `pkg = "x.y.z"` shape appears in both `hardhat-tests.toml` and `foundry-tests.toml` under `[build_systems]`; the `"pkg@x.y.z"` shape appears in `hardhat-tests.toml` `dependencies` arrays (foundry-tests.toml has no such arrays).

Track the solx-latest release pin feeding the integration harness's `solx-latest` toolchain. The version appears twice in the download URL (path segment and file name), hence two matchStrings; both occurrences resolve to the same dependency and are bumped together.

Keep the CI image's baked default toolchain in lockstep with rust-toolchain.toml: emits the same `rust` dependency as the built-in rust-toolchain manager, so both files land in one update PR. The ARG drifted 1.93.0→1.96.0 unnoticed before this manager existed.

packageRules.description: cargo: group minor/patch; majors stay individual
cargo: pre-1.0 crates treat 0.x→0.y as major (Cargo semver convention); ungroup from minor/patch so they land as individual PRs
dockerfile: group minor/patch; majors stay individual

Pinned git-rev cargo deps: inkwell/melior are forks tied to the LLVM 21.1 backend, slang_solidity_v2 is the WIP Slang v2 frontend revision bumped manually. Disable Renovate's default git-digest updates — these are bumped manually when feature/build compat warrants. Note: matches on depName (Cargo.toml key), not packageName (which is the git URL for git-source deps).

Pin mlir-sys to <220 (MLIR v21). solx-llvm is built against LLVM 21.1; mlir-sys 220 targets MLIR v22 which requires a coordinated solx-llvm migration to LLVM 22 first. solx-mlir/Cargo.toml carries a TODO to track mlir-sys 211 availability — the 211 line is what we want, not the 220 jump. PR #420 was the unwanted bump. Drop this rule once solx-llvm migrates to LLVM 22 (or once mlir-sys 211 ships and we move to it).
solx-ci-runner is a private GHCR package
Pin yarn to 1.x (Classic). Yarn 2+ (Berry) is a different product and the Hardhat/Foundry fixtures we run use Yarn 1 semantics; never auto-bump across that line.
The `hardhat@x.y.z` entries in solx-dev/hardhat-tests.toml are pinned Hardhat v2 fixtures; don't bump them across the v2→v3 line.
custom.regex deps only feed the integration harness; label so the gated suite runs.

solx-latest release pin: only the full benchmark matrix exercises the released binary, so bump PRs need that suite to prove the new release works in the harness. ci:integration is repeated because the workflow's label gate requires it alongside the full-matrix label.
